message:
fix(mcp): flush dirty storage on late-error paths in with_mutation

BeadsState::with_mutation used to:
  1. Open storage
  2. Run the mutation closure
  3. On Ok: auto-flush JSONL, return Ok(result)
  4. On Err: return Err(err) with NO flush

The step-4 path is the "late error" case: the closure mutated storage
(created/updated/closed an issue in SQLite), then returned Err — for
example, a tool with a side-effect-after-DB-write like comment sends
or label validation that runs post-insert. The DB change was durable
(SQLite committed), but the JSONL copy stayed dirty. On the next
`br sync`, the JSONL would look out-of-date relative to the DB, and
an operator running `br show` after the failed MCP call would see
the change while any `git diff .beads/issues.jsonl` reader would not.

New shape:

  - Capture dirty metadata before the closure runs.
  - On Err: re-read dirty metadata. If it differs from before (i.e.
    the closure actually committed something to SQLite before the
    error), run flush_dirty_storage() so the JSONL catches up.
  - On Ok: flush as before.

Extracted flush_dirty_storage as a helper so both paths use the same
implementation; sync::auto_flush plumbing is unchanged.

New regression test with_mutation_flushes_committed_changes_before_
returning_late_error creates an issue in the closure, then returns
an error. Asserts:
  - the error propagates (InvalidParams code intact)
  - the issue is in SQLite (create was durable)
  - dirty_issue_count == 0 (flush ran)
  - the JSONL file contains "id":"br-mcp-partial" (flush wrote it)

No change to the happy-path semantics or to the error code.
